source: .claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scenario-meanings.md → basic-turn (repeated three times in one interactive session)
notes: Three consecutive user prompts in one live codex REPL session. Unlike headless `codex exec` (single-shot, ends in process_exited), the interactive driver keeps the rollout session alive across all three turns, so the daemon observes three full ready→working→ready arcs on the SAME rollout session_id, then process_exited only when the driver tears the tmux REPL down. The daemon sees proc-PID pre-session born ready, then the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, then per-turn working/ready flips. Each turn's _done anchors to its own _start (not to session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not an earlier handoff. The per-turn TotalTokens accumulation the scenario validates isn't expressible in the phase DSL today (no metric form in the invariant DSL); it's covered by parser unit test TestParser_MultiTurnTokenCount_UsesPerTurnSnapshot.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
First user prompt → working on the rollout session
First turn ends → back to ready (state reset between turns)
Second user prompt → working on the same session
Second turn ends → back to ready
Third user prompt → working on the same session
Third turn ends in ready; session never enters waiting across the conversation
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session
